posts.text: "This month, Iran placed its biggest bet yet on its ability to control escalation. But this time, it appears headed toward calamity." bit.ly/4cXeuNs
RAND experts explain why a credible disarmament, demobilization, and reintegration program is the only realistic way forward for a lasting Israeli-Palestinian peace. bit.ly/4rZ3HGu

"This moment offers a rare chance to target a militia without making war on the Lebanese people or state. The strategic aim must be peace with Beirut, not a perpetual Lebanese front." nytimes.com/2026/03/19/opini…
"Even if Iran wins this war in the narrow sense of the term, its victory is bound to be a Pyrrhic one." bit.ly/3NtVcEY
"Israeli overreach in the campaign and failure to frame the fight as a war on Hezbollah but not on the state of Lebanon risks squandering a rare diplomatic opening." nytimes.com/2026/03/19/opini…
We examine how actors in the AI realm—states, firms, groups, and individuals—might promote beneficial AI development and mitigate risk through strategic cooperation. bit.ly/4bqFFOb

"Roll the dice enough times, and the odds eventually work against you," RAND's Raphael Cohen says of Tehran's "escalate to de-escalate" strategy.

"Iran is about to learn this lesson, and it will have only itself to blame." 
bit.ly/4rCCgBN

A prolonged Israeli operation in Lebanon could undermine Lebanon's institutions, turn its people against Israel, and further entrench Hezbollah's narrative, says RAND's Shira Efron.

"That's precisely the opposite of what Israel and the region need." nytimes.com/2026/03/19/opini…

The term "rural" is a kaleidoscope of conflicting definitions, says RAND's David Luckey. This lack of clarity "actively distorts resource allocation and could put the lives of rural Americans at risk." bit.ly/4uaeVdr

What are the potential implications of the Trump retirement accounts proposal?

Our experts used the RAND Budget Model to find out: bit.ly/4lkx9EM

There is significant uncertainty surrounding China's national security decisionmaking processes, especially in real time during a crisis or conflict.

This new primer can help reduce U.S. misperceptions about Chinese decisionmaking: bit.ly/4bhvawl
Our new study can help policymakers and analysts better understand the impacts of economic shocks in the United States. bit.ly/4s2gGb2
New survey: Most students who use AI for homework worry about its effects on critical thinking. bit.ly/4rWTWsh
This new primer can help policymakers better understand China's national security decisionmaking—and help determine U.S. responses during a crisis or a conflict. bit.ly/40pWcg9
"Improving rural policy begins with measuring rural realities more precisely." bit.ly/4rOFFOS

Our modeling of the Trump retirement accounts proposal finds that the program would be deficit neutral.

The program would pay for itself by reducing entitlement spending over 23 to 31 years, if assets count toward eligibility. bit.ly/4rpkhPa

What if you could see the economic effects of every line in the federal budget? The RAND Budget Model is making it possible.

Learn how in a new episode of the Policy Minded podcast: bit.ly/4rh2sll
This new report examines economic shocks in the United States, providing insights that can help anticipate recessions and develop appropriate policy responses. bit.ly/4uay3Yy

This new primer can help U.S. policymakers and analysts consider China’s informational picture, how Chinese decisionmakers tend to think about their environment, and who in the PRC has the authority to make which kinds of decisions. bit.ly/4sx8peW
